title: Mapping Words to Embeddings
concept: Loading pretrained Word2Vec or GloVe embeddings to represent words as semantic vectors.

exercises.prompt: What is the purpose of tokenizing text in sentiment analysis?

options.text: To split text into sentences and paragraphs.
To convert sentences into a format suitable for processing, like words or tokens.
To analyze the grammar of the text.

exercises.explain: Tokenizing is essential because it helps us break text into manageable parts, typically into words or tokens that can be analyzed.

cards.title: Understanding Word Representations
cards.body: Words need to be converted into numerical forms so that computers can understand and process them. This is where word embeddings come into play!

cards.title: What are Word Embeddings?
cards.body: Word embeddings are numerical representations of words. They capture the meanings of words based on the context in which they appear.

cards.title: Words as Points in Space
cards.body: Think of word embeddings like a map. Each word is a point on that map, and similar words are located closer together, just like cities that are near each other.

cards.title: Real-World Use of Word Embeddings
cards.body: Google's Word2Vec and GloVe are popular word embedding models that represent words in such a way that 'king' - 'man' + 'woman' results in a vector close to 'queen'.

cards.title: Myth: All Models Are the Same
cards.body: Not all word embeddings are created equal! Different models, like Word2Vec and GloVe, have different ways of understanding word relationships.

cards.title: Comparing Word Embeddings
cards.body: Here’s how Word2Vec and GloVe differ:

left.text: Learns representations from the local context of words.

exercises.prompt: What is the primary goal of using word embeddings like Word2Vec or GloVe?

options.text: To convert words into numerical vectors that capture meaning.
To store text in a video format.
To split text into punctuation and symbols.

exercises.explain: The main goal of word embeddings is to transform words into numerical vectors that allow us to capture their meanings and relationships.

exercises.prompt: True or False: Word embeddings can represent the meanings of words based on context.
exercises.explain: True! Word embeddings represent meanings based on the context in which words appear.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the blank: Word embeddings are numerical representations of ______.
exercises.sentence: Word embeddings are numerical representations of words.
exercises.explain: Words are the fundamental units that word embeddings represent as numerical values.

exercises.prompt: Which of the following is an example of a popular word embedding model?
exercises.explain: Word2Vec is a well-known word embedding model that converts words into numerical vectors.

exercises.prompt: Which statement about loading Word2Vec and GloVe embeddings is true?

options.text: They require large amounts of image data to process texts.
They are used for text data only and cannot be applied to other types.
They help words capture meaning based on context when loaded correctly.
They can only represent single words and not phrases.

exercises.explain: The correct answer is that loading Word2Vec and GloVe helps words capture meaning based on context, enabling effective analysis in tasks like sentiment detection.
